synthesis.status_map_markdown:
| Item | Plan V4 requirement | Status | Evidence | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **0A-1** | Secret-safe `baseline/manifest.json` (SHA + lock hash + OS + routing + seeds + commands + fixtures + per-secret SHA-256 digest+present-flag) | **MISSING** | No `baseline/manifest.json` exists; `baseline/` is entirely git-ignored (.gitignore:283). Only prose docs (baseline_findings.md, baseline_test_state.md). | Core proof-of-safety artifact never created; only narrative findings. |
| **0A-2** | Isolated sandbox N-run with per-output-artifact SHA-256; daemon paused | **MISSING** | Single exploratory pass at d76a0ea that crashed on hardcoded path; only SHA list is 10 source files, not run artifacts. | No hashed run artifacts; no isolated N-run. |
| **0A-3** | Governing RACE/faithfulness characterized (mean+spread+equivalence band, N≥3); un-pinnable oracle = BLOCKING stop | **WRONG** | Only N=2 unit runs (1343p/104f/103e). grep for "RACE score"/"equivalence band" in review_readiness = empty. Harness documented broken (THIN positive control fires 0 searches, cause "indeterminate"); initiative proceeded anyway. | Governing oracle never characterized and never treated as the BLOCKING stop the plan mandates. Inverts the one inviolable rule. |
| **0A-4** | Written comparison protocol (regression vs noise) | **MISSING** | grep "comparison protocol"/"regression vs noise"/"equivalence band" across docs = no matches. | No declared-before-change protocol. |
| **0A-5** | Characterize all 3 graph selectors (v1/v2/v3) as replay fixtures | **MISSING** | No graph_v2/v3/PG_GRAPH_VERSION fixtures under baseline/ or review_readiness. | Not started; also a 3C precondition. |
| **0B SHA-pin** | SHA-pin all 28 CI actions | **DONE** | 325f90e: 0 unpinned refs, 33 pinned to 40-hex SHAs across 11 workflows (≥ target). | — |
| **0B attribution** | Fix third-party attribution | **DONE** | THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md, hardened over 4 codex rounds; clean-room STORM/FS-Researcher provenance. | — |
| **0B pyproject+CI** | pyproject.toml + CI in report-only mode | **DONE** | 0835b1a pyproject; python_ci.yml `continue-on-error:true` + `|| true`, header "Phase 0: REPORT-ONLY"; SHA-pinned pre-commit. | — |
| **0B README/arch** | Tidy README/architecture | **PARTIAL** | README rebranded (5c78cc0); architecture.md untouched (empty diff). | Architecture half unaddressed. |
| **Oracle Layer-1** | Deterministic oracle mapping to 0A | **PARTIAL** | cassette.py/llm_cassette.py, 12 passing tests, byte-diff demo; registry lazy-init fix. | Toy 2-call demo; not wired to acceptance harness; harness still hardcodes `/workspace/` (lines 28,281-282); does not pin governing measurement. registry.py = runtime-code change in 0B. |
| **1A** | Classify + own ALL ~923 keys, per-key owner + precedence; gate = 100% classified+owned before any migration | **PARTIAL** | 1044 keys 100% classified into 6 categories (config_keys_classified.json, gitignored). Verified: 0/1044 have owner; 0/1044 have precedence. Only blanket-owner note. | Ownership + precedence legs unmet; migration proceeded before own-gate satisfied. |
| **1B** | Full-behavior matrix: {unset,empty,valid,malformed}×{default/.env/process-env/CLI}×{case}×{timing}×{type str/coerced/SecretStr} | **PARTIAL** | 871-key registry tested for unset/empty/override==os.getenv only; 12 model keys add timing+case. | Malformed, precedence-layering (.env/CLI never exercised), tail case/timing, coercion-type, SecretStr all untested. Proves DEFAULT resolution only. |
| **1C order** | One module at a time; acceptance harness after each | **PARTIAL** | First slices per-module (6d8a733, f746662); then dd96ceb = 832 sites / 130 files in ONE codemod. No acceptance/RACE mention in any Phase-1 commit. | Bulk codemod, not one-at-a-time; harness never run (import/collection/characterization proof instead). Score-safe by construction. |
| **1C SecretStr** | SecretStr for secrets, call-sites unwrapped | **MISSING** | grep SecretStr src/ = 0 matches. 6+ API-key secrets sit as plain str (empty defaults) in registry; explicitly deferred. | Entirely unstarted; no type, no design doc. No value leak (defaults ''). |
| **1C conflicts** | Byte-identical value preserved; conflicting defaults handled safely | **PARTIAL** | config_conflicts.md documents 20 conflicting-default keys, incl. PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD 0.65 vs 0.75 (faithfulness-critical); correctly excluded from registry. | 20 conflicts documented but UNRESOLVED — a live path-dependent faithfulness divergence remains. 512 os.getenv reads across 133 files unmigrated; promised CI grep-ban not implemented. |
| **2A** | Public-compatibility inventory before any rename/delete | **MISSING** | No inventory artifact; no phase2 branch; docs "phase2" hits belong to unrelated older gate initiative. | Not started; blocks 2B. |
| **2B** | Execute 210-rename worklist by risk class | **PARTIAL** | Worklist exists, counts match plan EXACTLY (210 RENAME = 105 SAFE/12 TEXT/45 FILE/32 ALIAS/16 DOMAIN; 135 KEEP). But git-untracked ('??') in polaris_project, uncommitted to branch; ZERO renames executed (symbols/files still present). | Worklist correct but not committed and not executed; blocked by missing 2A. |
| **2C** | Turn on generation checkpoint (pre-check data only, gated write, byte-identical) | **MISSING** | checkpoint_manager.py pre-exists (old 2b42bc5); PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED defaults '0'; only touch is Phase-1C resolve() swap. | Not started; still disabled by default. |
| **3A** | Fill ~530 docstrings + ADRs | **MISSING** | No docstring-fill commit; zero ADRs (find = nothing). | Unstarted. |
| **3B** | Flakiness policy before required CI; hash-pinning; license/secret scanning | **MISSING** | CI still report-only (correct per gate); no flakiness bar/quarantine; `--hash=` count in requirements.lock = 0; no pip-audit/bandit/gitleaks/detect-secrets. | Prerequisites all absent. |
| **3C** | Graph fork removal fully gated (5 gates) | **MISSING** | Fork intact; __init__.py still routes on PG_GRAPH_VERSION; only edit is Phase-1C resolve() swap. None of 5 gates exist; 0A-5 precondition also absent. | Unstarted. |
| **S1** | Config governance 100% classified+owned+precedence | **PARTIAL** | Strong byte-identical migration + classification; but 0/1044 owner, 0/1044 precedence. | 2 of 3 acceptance legs unmet. |
| **S2** | Public-compat inventory + supported-Python/install/state-migration/rollback doc | **MISSING** | No artifact in review-readiness commit range; supported_scope.md predates initiative (2b42bc5, 2026-07-07). | Entirely absent; gates Phase 2. |
| **S3** | Test quality / flakiness policy | **PARTIAL** | baseline_test_state.md: 16,501 collected, 2x deterministic run; import-time root cause fixed. | N=2 (< N≥3); no quarantine list, no bar, no governing characterization; 104f+103e untriaged. |
| **S4** | Security/privacy threat model (API auth, SSRF, prompt-injection, PII, log redaction, checkpoint retention) | **MISSING** | No S4 artifact; pre-existing threat-model docs predate initiative (2b42bc5). | Entirely absent; gates external exposure. |
| **S5** | Operational readiness checklist | **MISSING** | No S5 artifact; runbook.md/observability docs predate initiative (2b42bc5). | Entirely absent; gates external exposure. |
| **S6** | Reproducible build (two builds hash-match + hash-pinned deps + locked env + SBOM) | **MISSING** | Only version-pinned lock (0 `--hash=` lines); no SBOM; no two-build/diffoscope job. | 3 of 4 legs absent; "reproducible" cannot be claimed. |

next_step.what: STOP forward work and pin the governing oracle: first fix the acceptance harness's hardcoded-path crash, then run acceptance_outline_agent.py N>=3 in an isolated sandbox to statistically characterize the governing RACE score + faithfulness verdicts (mean+spread+declared equivalence band). If it cannot be characterized in a stable band, that is the plan's BLOCKING stop — investigate variance, do not proceed. Only once the oracle is pinned, emit the secret-safe baseline/manifest.json (0A-1), the written comparison protocol (0A-4), and the 3-selector replay fixtures (0A-5).
next_step.why_next: Plan V4 safety rule 1 is absolute: 'no runtime change before the 0A secret-safe manifest + comparison protocol + N-run baseline exist, with the governing measurement statistically characterized (or execution blocked).' 0A-3 is currently WRONG (harness documented broken, positive control fires 0 searches, only N=2 unit runs, no RACE characterization) and the initiative already shipped an 832-site runtime codemod (dd96ceb) and typed-settings migration on top of this un-pinned, admitted-broken oracle. Every 'we did not move the score' claim in Phase 1 rests on an oracle the team's own docs call 'not a trustworthy oracle yet.' This is the single highest-priority correction and it gates everything downstream. It also unblocks 0A-5, which is the precondition for 3C.
next_step.execution_outline: 1) Fix acceptance_outline_agent.py portability: replace the four hardcoded /workspace/ paths (lines 28, 31, 281-282) with repo-relative / env-configurable paths so the harness runs in this worktree. 2) Diagnose the THIN positive-control failure (0 searches when search_more_evidence expected) — determine if the harness is vacuous or the scenario is misconfigured; do not proceed until the positive control demonstrably fires. 3) In an isolated scratch dir with the GitHub backup daemon paused, run the harness N>=3 (offline/cassette-replay path preferred to avoid provider nondeterminism — the Layer-1 cassette already exists and should be wired in here), capturing RACE score + faithfulness verdict sets + per-artifact SHA-256 each run. 4) Compute mean+spread; declare the equivalence band; if unstable, halt and record the BLOCKING condition. 5) Write baseline/manifest.json (git-ignored, documented access): commit SHA, requirements.lock hash, Python/OS, model routing, seeds, exact commands, fixtures, and per-secret SHA-256 digest + present/absent flag (never raw values). 6) Write the comparison-protocol doc: 'same' = RACE within band AND identical faithfulness verdict sets AND artifact-hash match; define regression vs noise. 7) Capture v1/v2/v3 selector replay fixtures (0A-5). 8) Retroactively re-validate the already-committed Phase-1 codemod against this now-pinned oracle before any further Phase-1/2/3 work.

missed_or_wrong: 0A-3 (governing measurement) is WRONG, not merely incomplete: the acceptance harness is documented as broken (THIN positive control fires 0 searches, cause 'indeterminate'), only N=2 unit-test runs were recorded (< N>=3), and NO RACE-score mean/spread/equivalence band was ever computed — yet the initiative proceeded to ship runtime changes. This inverts the plan's one inviolable rule.

The secret-safe baseline/manifest.json (0A-1) — the plan's explicit starting point and the foundation of every 'score unchanged' claim — was never created. baseline/ is entirely git-ignored (line 283), so even the classified JSON is untracked.
No written comparison protocol (0A-4) and no isolated N-run with per-artifact SHA-256 (0A-2) exist. The only 'run' crashed on a hardcoded /workspace path.

The acceptance harness's own portability crash is UNFIXED: acceptance_outline_agent.py still hardcodes /workspace/outline_agent_wt at lines 28, 281-282 (and /workspace/POLARIS/.env at 31). This is the prerequisite that blocks pinning the oracle, and it was identified but left unrepaired.

Per-key ownership and precedence (1A / S1) were skipped entirely: 0 of 1044 classified entries carry an owner or precedence field. Migration proceeded on a blanket-owner note, violating the '100% classified + owned before a single key migrates' gate.
SecretStr (1C) is entirely unstarted — zero occurrences in src/; 6+ API-key secrets sit as plain str in the registry; no design doc for the deferred pass.

20 conflicting-default keys were surfaced but left UNRESOLVED, including PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD (0.65 vs 0.75) — a live, path-dependent divergence in the faithfulness gate that the plan's frozen-faithfulness invariant is supposed to forbid. Correctly excluded from the byte-identical migration, but the divergence remains live and unowned.
The 210-name rename worklist, though correct, is git-untracked in the main project dir and was never committed to a review-readiness branch; zero renames executed.
512 os.getenv reads across 133 files remain unmigrated and the promised 'CI grep-ban on new os.getenv' guard was never implemented.
architecture.md (0B) never tidied; only README rebranded.
S2, S4, S5, S6 are entirely absent from the initiative; docs that superficially match all predate it (I-deepfix-001, commit 2b42bc5, 2026-07-07).
planv4_deviations: 1C 'migrate one module at a time, acceptance harness after each module' -> actual was a BULK AST codemod of 832 sites across 130 files in ONE commit (dd96ceb), and the acceptance harness was NEVER run (equivalence proven via import-cleanliness + pytest --collect-only + characterization tests instead). SCORE-SAFE BY CONSTRUCTION (resolve(k)==os.getenv(k,default), coercion wrappers int()/float()/=='1' untouched at call sites, env names verbatim, registry default==exact literal test-proven over 871 keys) — but off-process, and unverifiable against the real pipeline because the oracle is un-pinned.

1B 'full-behavior matrix' -> actual coverage is byte-identical DEFAULT-resolution only (unset/empty/override for 871 keys; timing+case for 12 model keys). The precedence axis (.env/process-env/CLI), malformed input, tail case/timing, coercion-type, and SecretStr axes are untested. NOT a score-safety violation (defaults are proven identical), but it does not constitute the 'real byte-identical proof' the plan says 1B must be.

0B 'touches NO importable runtime code' -> registry.py (audit_ir) was made lazy-init across 4 commits (43214a2..0780157) — a change to importable runtime code inside a phase framed as touching none. Justified as a portability/collection fix; low risk but a literal deviation from the 0B scope boundary.

Safety rule 1 'baseline first' -> DEVIATED and NOT score-safe in principle: all Phase-1 runtime work was performed before the 0A manifest/protocol/characterized-oracle existed. Byte-identical-by-construction arguments substitute for the missing empirical baseline, but the plan explicitly forbids proceeding on an un-pinned oracle.

S1 headline count: plan says ~923 keys; work classified 1044 runtime keys (superset, defensible) out of a 1644 full surface (600 scripts-only keys excluded). A scope framing difference, not a safety issue.
synthesis.phase0_blocking: YES — later work is standing on an un-pinned baseline, and this is the single most serious finding. Plan V4 safety rule 1 forbids any runtime change before the 0A secret-safe manifest + comparison protocol + N-run baseline exist WITH the governing RACE/faithfulness measurement statistically characterized (or execution blocked). None of those exist: baseline/manifest.json is MISSING (0A-1), the comparison protocol is MISSING (0A-4), the isolated hashed N-run is MISSING (0A-2), and the governing measurement was never characterized (0A-3 WRONG) — worse, the acceptance harness is documented as broken (positive control fires 0 searches, cause 'indeterminate') and still crashes on hardcoded /workspace paths. Despite this, the initiative shipped an 832-site runtime codemod (dd96ceb) plus the typed-settings migration. Those changes are byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (a strong static argument) and no plaintext secret leaked, so there is no concrete evidence the score moved — but the plan's whole point is that 'byte-identical by construction' must be BACKED by an empirical pinned oracle before shipping, precisely because construction arguments can miss coercion/timing/path-dependent effects. The un-pinnable oracle should have been treated as a BLOCKING stop; instead the initiative continued. Every downstream 'we did not move the score' claim currently rests on an oracle the team's own docs call 'not a trustworthy oracle yet.' Phase 0-A must be completed and the committed Phase-1 changes retroactively validated against the pinned oracle before any further phase proceeds.

codex_points: Codex confirms the core diagnosis: Phase 0A is blocking and no existing runtime change is review-ready. But the audit's chosen next_step order is WRONG.

ORDERING FLAW (the central correction): '0A-3-then-0A-1' reverses Plan V4. You must FIRST identify and manifest the last PRE-runtime-change commit (apparently before 43214a2), THEN characterize it. Running N>=3 on the already-modified HEAD only creates a NEW baseline; it cannot prove preservation of the original score. The manifest (0A-1) must anchor to the pre-change commit before any characterization.

Retro-validation SCOPE is too narrow: validation must cover EVERY runtime change — the four 0B registry.py lazy-init commits, the early per-module settings slices, AND dd96ceb — not merely the Phase-1 codemod the audit named.

Aggregate retro-validation CANNOT cure the 1C violation. The 832-site single-commit codemod stays noncompliant; migrations must be replayed module-by-module with acceptance evidence after each — a bulk re-validation does not satisfy 'one module at a time'.
The toy two-call Layer-1 cassette cannot substitute for the governing harness until full-harness live-vs-replay equivalence is demonstrated; the audit leans on it prematurely.

A git-ignored, local-only manifest is INADEQUATE as independent-review evidence — the secret-safe manifest and results need durable, reviewer-accessible storage. (The audit accepted 'git-ignored, documented access' as sufficient.)

'Faithfulness verdict SETS' are insufficient — sets discard fixture identity, ordering, and multiplicity. Record keyed PER-FIXTURE verdicts + governing scores. This tightens the audit's comparison-protocol definition.

STATUS DOWNGRADES codex demands: 1A and 1C-order should be WRONG/VIOLATED, not PARTIAL; 2B should be MISSING/NOT-STARTED (zero renames ran), not PARTIAL; S3 should be MISSING (no flakiness policy exists), not PARTIAL.
0B attribution is NOT proven DONE by a notices file + 'four codex rounds' alone — completeness and license-obligation coverage need explicit evidence. Codex downgrades this from the audit's DONE.

'Score-safe by construction' and 'no value leak' are UNSUPPORTED claims given the unpinned oracle, incomplete behavior matrix, and plaintext runtime secret types — the audit repeats these as mitigations but they cannot be asserted yet.

The conflicting faithfulness defaults (PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD 0.65 vs 0.75) are a migration BLOCKER, but their pre-existence is NOT itself evidence THIS initiative changed faithfulness; they must remain frozen until the oracle is established. (Refines the audit's framing.)

Codex's better next step: freeze all work; establish the pre-runtime commit + clean environment; create 0A-1 anchored there; repair/version the harness OUTSIDE production runtime and validate its controls; complete 0A-2/3/4/5 WITHOUT inspecting post-change outcomes when declaring the equivalence band; THEN reconstruct the runtime work through Plan V4's required gates.
gate.confirmed_next_step: Freeze all forward work. Do NOT run the harness on HEAD first. Order (codex-corrected): (1) Identify and check out the last pre-runtime-change commit (the parent of 43214a2, i.e. before the 0B registry.py lazy-init changes), and write the secret-safe baseline/manifest.json anchored to THAT commit — stored in durable, reviewer-accessible (not merely git-ignored/local) location, secrets as SHA-256 digest+present-flag only. (2) Repair the acceptance harness's four hardcoded /workspace/ paths (acceptance_outline_agent.py lines 28,31,281,282) in a copy OUTSIDE production runtime, and validate its controls — diagnose why the THIN positive control fires 0 searches before trusting it. (3) Complete 0A-2 (isolated N>=3 run with per-artifact SHA-256, daemon paused), 0A-3 (statistically characterize governing RACE + PER-FIXTURE keyed faithfulness verdicts, mean+spread+declared equivalence band; un-pinnable = BLOCKING stop), 0A-4 (written comparison protocol), and 0A-5 (v1/v2/v3 selector replay fixtures) — all declared WITHOUT peeking at post-change outcomes. (4) Only then reconstruct the runtime work: retro-validate EVERY runtime change (0B registry.py commits + early settings slices + dd96ceb) against the pinned oracle, and replay the 1C migration MODULE-BY-MODULE with acceptance evidence after each — aggregate re-validation does not cure the bulk-codemod violation. Keep the conflicting faithfulness defaults frozen throughout.

summary: Codex-gated audit of all committed work against Plan V4: find missed/wrong/skipped items and the correct next step

phases.detail: codex adversarially verifies the audit + next-step decision
